category_title: Elementary student with ASD who uses visual supports and exhibits avoidance behaviors like elopement and crawling under tables
minimal_context: Evan is transitioning to a one-on-one work area to complete five addition and five subtraction problems.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Evan, it is time to transition to the work area. We need to write the answers to five addition and five subtraction problems with two-digit numerals.

STUDENT: (Does not follow the direction to move. Stands in place and flips a straw back and forth in front of his eyes.)

TUTOR: Please walk over to the table so we can begin your math assignment.

STUDENT: (Drops down and lies flat on the floor instead of walking to the structured task area.)

TUTOR: You need to get up off the floor and sit at the table to do your work.

STUDENT: (Crawls under the table to avoid the activity and begins kicking at the educator's legs.)

options.A: Allow the use of a calculator alongside the math assignment; read the addition or subtraction problem, enter it into the calculator, and then write the answer.
options.B: Present a visual schedule that identifies the activities of the day and the reinforcers he is eligible to receive for contingent performance during the session.
options.C: Provide clues and requests for clarification regarding the addition and subtraction problems while the student is under the table during the one-on-one work time.
options.D: Respond by saying, "Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going with the math assignment during this session."
